pages.raw_text:
The Washington Water Power
company has granted its carmen an
increase in wages of l cent per
hour. The company was compelled
to make this concession to their
employees on account of the trouble
it has had lately in keeping its
men. There has been no difficulty
in securing men but there has been
in keeping them. The men would
work long enough to break in and
receive about one months pay and
then quit. The reason for this was
that they soon learned that they
could make more money working
on a farm than they could ON the
cars of the local monopoly and
work less hours. The men asked for
an increase of cents per hour,
but will have to be contented
with 1.


From the mems standpoint, the
compromise was secured only
through the prevailing scarcity OF
men and the coming rush during
the fair.
